chunks.text: EECS researchers win MLArchSys Best Paper Award. EECS Professors Alvin Cheung and Sophia Shao, and their students Charles Hong and Sahil Batia, have won the Best Paper Award at the Machine Learning for Computer Architecture and Systems (MLArchSys) Workshop, held at ISCA 2025. Their award-winning paper, titled 'Autocomp: LLM-Driven Code Optimization for Tensor Accelerators', presents a novel approach to optimizing code for tensor accelerators using large language models.
chunks.question: Who won the MLArchSys Best Paper Award and for what paper?
chunks.answer: Alvin Cheung, Sophia Shao, students for Autocomp.

chunks.text: EECS Researchers win Most Influential Paper Award from Design Automation Conference. EECS researchers Jonathan Bachrach, John Wawrzynek, Krste Asanovic, and their students and collaborators Huy Vo, Brian Richards, Yunsup Lee, Andrew Waterman, and Rimas Aviženis have received the Design Automation Conference’s (DAC) Most Influential Paper Award for the decade 2010–2020. Their groundbreaking paper, 'Chisel: Constructing Hardware in a Scala Embedded Language' (2012), introduced a new language for hardware design that has significantly influenced the field.
chunks.question: What award did EECS researchers receive for the Chisel paper?
chunks.answer: DAC Most Influential Paper Award for 2010-2020.

chunks.text: Sagar Karandikar wins 2025 ACM SIGARCH/IEEE CS TCCA Outstanding Dissertation Award. EECS Assistant Professor Sagar Karandikar has been awarded the 2025 ACM SIGARCH/IEEE CS TCCA Outstanding Dissertation Award. This prestigious honor recognizes exceptional doctoral research in the field of computer architecture. Karandikar was recognized for advancing agile hardware/software co-design tools and deploying them at a hyperscaler to architect custom solutions.
chunks.question: What award did Sagar Karandikar win and for what contribution?
chunks.answer: Outstanding Dissertation Award for agile co-design tools.

chunks.text: EECS researchers win Best Robot Learning Paper Award at IEEE ICRA 2025. Professors Ken Goldberg, John Kubiatowicz, and their team of students and collaborators: Kaiyuan Chen, Letian Fu, David Huang, Yanxiang Zhang, Lawrence Yunliang Chen, Huang Huang, Kush Hari, Ashwin Balakrishna, Ted Xiao, and Pannag R Sanketi, have received the Best Robot Learning Paper Award at the IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2025. This award highlights their significant contributions to robot learning research.
chunks.question: Who won the Best Robot Learning Paper Award at ICRA 2025?
chunks.answer: Ken Goldberg, John Kubiatowicz, and their team.

chunks.text: Prabal Dutta named Ohio State Distinguished Alumni. Prabal Dutta, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, has been awarded the Distinguished Alumni Award for Academic Excellence from the College of Engineering at The Ohio State University. This award honors alumni who have demonstrated significant achievement in academia through contributions to teaching, research, or education.
chunks.question: What award did Prabal Dutta receive and from where?
chunks.answer: Distinguished Alumni Award from Ohio State University.

chunks.text: Berkeley engineers develop customizable, 3D-printed robot for tech newbies. As an undergraduate student, Yufeng Chi (B.S.’23 EECS) was captivated by humanoid and legged robots. Eager to learn more, he would watch YouTube videos and dive into class projects, but getting hands-on experience and tinkering on his own was not easy. He was interested in building his own version of a robot, leading to the development of a customizable, 3D-printed robot designed to be accessible for beginners in technology.
chunks.question: What did Yufeng Chi develop for tech beginners?

chunks.text: Fast Company: This Perplexity cofounder wants to help AI breakthroughs graduate from university labs. Andy Konwinski (CS Ph.D. 2012), with help from Google’s Jeff Dean and other AI luminaries, has launched a new institute to help turn promising academic AI breakthroughs into open-source tools, startups, and real-world products while fostering an 'AI for good' research culture.
chunks.question: What is Andy Konwinski's goal with his new institute?
chunks.answer: Turn academic AI into real-world products.

chunks.text: May the Best Bot Win. Started as student side project, Chatbot Arena is now the AI industry’s scoreboard—and a $100 million startup. It provides a platform for evaluating and comparing AI chatbots, becoming a key resource in the industry and achieving significant financial success.
chunks.question: What is Chatbot Arena and its current status?
chunks.answer: AI chatbot scoreboard, now a $100M startup.

chunks.text: Michael Harrison has passed away. EECS Professor Emeritus Michael A. Harrison passed away on Saturday, June 7. He was 89. Professor Harrison earned his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Michigan in 1963. That same year, he joined the University of California, Berkeley, as an assistant professor and spent most of his academic career there, contributing significantly to the field.
chunks.question: Who was Michael Harrison and when did he pass away?
chunks.answer: EECS Professor Emeritus, passed away June 7.

chunks.text: Dan Garcia and Brian Harvey win the ACM Karl V. Karlstrom Outstanding Educator Award. EECS Teaching Professors Dan Garcia and Brian Harvey have been awarded the Karl V. Karlstrom Outstanding Educator Award, the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) announced today in a press release. Garcia and Harvey were recognized for their advocacy of and advances in education to bring the beauty and joy of computing to a wider audience.
chunks.question: What award did Dan Garcia and Brian Harvey win?
chunks.answer: ACM Karl V. Karlstrom Outstanding Educator Award.
